title: test: disable artifacts cache with composefs
body:
layers restored from a tarball won't be converted to composefs (for now at least), so disable the cache when using composefs.

comments.body: Was this supposed to fix the lxgetattr flake? It does not seem to: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5139484897968128/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html

> Was this supposed to fix the lxgetattr flake? It does not seem to: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5139484897968128/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html

no, I am still trying to find a reproducer.
@edsantiago here are some of the failing tests: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23274/checks?check_run_id=27476648865
OIC. Now I see what `NO_TEST_CACHE` does. That... is not a solution. We can't run CI like that. If that's the only way to get CI to pass, let's just disable composefs.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

tests seem to pass now, but we need these three fixes in storage:

- https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/2031
- https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/2036
- https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/2037

@edsantiago If you'd like to test with the potential fix for the flake,  I've prepared a branch that includes all the fixes above: https://github.com/giuseppe/storage/tree/test-composefs-fixes-for-podman

images copied to the RW store with `AddImageToRWStore` won't be stored in composefs mode.  This may change in future, if we automatically convert images not pulled from a registry.  For now I think it is desiderable behavior so we can test both variants: images stored in composefs mode in the additional store, and images stored in the traditional format when imported.

ready for review.

@edsantiago PTAL

Thanks @giuseppe. This is a lot of scariness to review, I'm really uncomfortable with some of the changes, so I've just been hammering away in #17831. I just saw [this failure](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6664205737132032/html/int-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-commit-podman-commit-adds-exposed-ports--1). Is it composefs?
```
# podman-remote [options] run --name testcon -p 8585:80 quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest true
# podman-remote [options] commit -q testcon newimage
Error: copying layers and metadata for container "cecc9c90f4107cf89808b8234df98e173e1bf052a006665f460a1e86f8d070b8": initializing source containers-storage:testcon: extracting layer "1745071114b3933b11b73a9f099d2b82d4e80262c1211b1b6e7f388466ebdf3c": invalid argument
```

> Thanks @giuseppe. This is a lot of scariness to review, I'm really uncomfortable with some of the changes, so I've just been hammering away in #17831. I just saw [this failure](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6664205737132032/html/int-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-commit-podman-commit-adds-exposed-ports--1). Is it composefs?

difficult to say, commit doesn't create a composefs image but it still access the layers stored in composefs from the additional image store.  I've not seen that error before

Another one, rawhide, [on commit](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6461293362675712/html/int-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-commit-podman-commit-container-check-env-variables--1). I'm not saying this is composefs, but this is a flake I've never seen before today.

Also another new flake, but I'll file a new issue for that.

> This makes me super uncomfortable. It seems like a kludgy workaround for bugs in composefs, and I worry that some day someone else may use it to work around other future bugs. And this only works because of the new local registry; prior to that it would pull from the net, which would be slow and flaky.

the images are pulled only once, and stored in the additional image store so it would still work pulling them from the network.

It is a workaround for composefs, since it supports only pulling from a registry at the moment.  We might need to support pulling from other sources to enable "conversion" of images.  That was not initially needed for partial pulls, so it is something we still need to implement.

> The commit stack is also deeply troubling: I had a lot of trouble reviewing commit-by-commit because work in one is undone in the next. If this is to be approved, may I encourage you to give some thought to cleaning up the individual commits?

I've not noticed that these pieces were left in the second commit.  Dropped them
Cockpit tests failed for commit 1ab3e1ce2b66cc48ced3710633d4a4a4f88dc5c4. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

Wrt. the `chpasswd` [breakage in rawhide](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/2eee4823-901e-44c9-9ae3-f8b58b4bd341/): This was fallout from an intermediate bad shadow-utils upload in rawhide. See  https://github.com/stratis-storage/stratisd/pull/3623#issuecomment-2246827933 ff. for details.

TL/DR: Please retry the failed tests here and in #23275 and other recent PRs. Thanks!
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
Cockpit tests failed for commit 464b01d67755bfff6f76835083902f3234fce599. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

New flake (as in, one I've never seen before, and is happening on [rawhide root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5036223750733824/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-create-image-with-running-container--1):
```
# podman [options] --pull-option=enable_partial_images=true --pull-option=convert_images=true container checkpoint --create-image alpine-checkpoint-bmjmtt --keep e3793c9b35baf7d6a62a976fb4da4e4fc139c535edd159cd6edcf96a8b4e1d02
Error: exporting root file-system diff for "e3793c9b35baf7d6a62a976fb4da4e4fc139c535edd159cd6edcf96a8b4e1d02": invalid argument
```
I'm blaming composefs. Should I open a new issue for it?

yes please, I think it is the same root issue as https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23274#issuecomment-2243363751

EDIT: you could open it directly in c/storage, it definitely looks like composefs
https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/124
Should we then not merge for now given there is a (new?) flake? Or does this fix enough other flakes/problems that this should be merged regardless?

Speaking as the person running the no-flake-retry tests, this is a big improvement over the current state of composefs in podman. With this PR in #17831, I see fewer flakes. Still see flakes, and I fully expect there to be more, but as-is right now composefs is unusable. Incremental progress is ok with me.
That said, if the EINVAL flake is an easy one to find/fix, it'd sure be nice to whack it
without this PR we are not creating the files in the additional image store in the composefs format
LGTM

let's wait for https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/2043 and https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/2044 before merging.  Hopefully they can help to troubleshoot the "invalid argument" flake
patches for c/storage included, ready to merge
